title: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Ex-Offenders - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Transitional Housing/Shelter * Ex-Offenders 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Disabilities Issues Ex-Offenders Native American Community AIDS/HIV Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 6505 Headquarter Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 354-8014 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY YAMHILL COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 28380 NE Wilsonville Road Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 560-9387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHELTERCARE SHELTERCARE SHORT TERM HOUSING SHORT TERM HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short term housing support and shelter. Housing term ranges from 3 months to 2 years depending on individual circumstances. Clients are connected with a care team who help them connect to resources, learn skills, and find permanent housing. Programs include: -ShelterCare Medical Recuperation -Frequent Users Systems Engagement (FUSE) 969 Highway 99N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Adults who are medically fragile, chronically homeless, or frequent users of hospitals and criminal justice systems Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 686-1262 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY YAMHILL COUNTY SHELTERCARE SHORT TERM HOUSING
